name: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive
issuingBody: European Parliament and Council of the European Union

scope.description: Phased application based on company size and turnover

phases.notes: Companies with 5000+ employees AND €1,500M+ net worldwide turnover
Companies with 3000+ employees AND €900M+ net worldwide turnover
Companies with 1000+ employees AND €450M+ net worldwide turnover

nonEUCompanies.description: Non-EU companies generating turnover in the EU above applicable thresholds

financialSector.note: Financial sector initially excluded from value chain due diligence obligations; review clause by 2030

valueChainScope.description: Chain of activities covering own operations, subsidiaries, and business partners

tiers.description: Subsidiaries over which the company exercises control
Direct and indirect business partners in the chain of activities
tiers.scope: Upstream (design, extraction, sourcing, manufacture, transport, storage, supply) and downstream (distribution, transport, storage) — downstream limited to disposal for own products

dueDiligenceObligations.description: Six-step due diligence framework per Articles 5-16

steps.name: Integrating due diligence into policies and management systems
steps.description: Adopt and implement a due diligence policy containing a description of the approach, a code of conduct for employees and subsidiaries, and a description of verification processes.
requirements: Due diligence policy adopted at company level
Code of conduct describing rules and principles for employees and subsidiaries
Description of processes to implement due diligence including verification measures
Policy updated annually and whenever significant change occurs

steps.name: Identifying and assessing actual and potential adverse impacts
steps.description: Take appropriate measures to identify and assess actual and potential adverse human rights and environmental impacts arising from own operations, subsidiaries, and business partners.
requirements: Map own operations, subsidiaries, and business partner activities
Identify and assess actual and potential adverse impacts
Use appropriate resources including independent reports, risk databases
Consult affected stakeholders and their representatives
Prioritise based on severity and likelihood (Article 9)

steps.name: Preventing and mitigating potential adverse impacts
steps.description: Take appropriate measures to prevent or adequately mitigate potential adverse impacts that have been or should have been identified.
requirements: Develop and implement a prevention action plan with timelines
Seek contractual assurances from direct business partners
Make necessary investments (e.g., management or production processes)
Provide targeted and proportionate support to SME business partners
Collaborate with other entities including industry initiatives
Suspend or terminate business relationship as last resort if prevention fails

steps.name: Bringing actual adverse impacts to an end
steps.description: Take appropriate measures to bring actual adverse impacts to an end and to minimise the extent of such impacts.
requirements: Neutralise or minimise the extent of adverse impacts
Develop and implement a corrective action plan with timelines
Seek contractual assurances from direct business partners
Provide targeted and proportionate support to SME business partners
Provide remediation where the company caused or jointly caused the impact
Suspend or terminate business relationship as last resort

steps.name: Establishing and maintaining a complaints procedure
steps.description: Establish a procedure enabling persons and organisations to submit complaints regarding actual or potential adverse impacts.
requirements: Accessible to affected persons, trade unions, civil society organisations
Fair, publicly available, and transparent procedure
Reasonable timeframes for response
Right to request follow-up and meet with company representatives
Complainants protected from retaliation

steps.name: Monitoring the effectiveness of due diligence
steps.description: Carry out periodic assessments of own operations, subsidiaries, and business partners to monitor the effectiveness of the identification, prevention, mitigation, bringing to an end, and minimisation of adverse impacts.
requirements: Periodic assessments at least every 12 months
Ad-hoc assessments when there are reasonable grounds to believe new risks have arisen
Qualitative and quantitative indicators
Verification by independent third-party where appropriate
Update due diligence policy and measures based on assessment results

humanRights.description: Adverse impacts on human rights as listed in Part I Section 1 of the Annex

internationalInstruments.name: International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights
International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights
ILO Forced Labour Convention (No. 29)
keyRights: prohibition of forced or compulsory labour

internationalInstruments.name: ILO Freedom of Association and Protection of the Right to Organise Convention (No. 87)
ILO Right to Organise and Collective Bargaining Convention (No. 98)
ILO Equal Remuneration Convention (No. 100)
keyRights: equal remuneration for work of equal value

internationalInstruments.name: ILO Abolition of Forced Labour Convention (No. 105)
ILO Worst Forms of Child Labour Convention (No. 182)
keyRights: prohibition of worst forms of child labour

internationalInstruments.name: Convention on the Rights of the Child

environmental.description: Adverse environmental impacts as listed in Part I Section 2 and Part II of the Annex

civilLiability.description: Member States shall ensure that companies are liable for damages if they intentionally or negligently fail to comply with due diligence obligations and an adverse impact that should have been identified, prevented, mitigated, brought to an end, or its extent minimised through appropriate measures results in damage to a natural or legal person.
provisions: Companies liable for damage caused by failure to comply with Articles 10-12
Full compensation for damages including lost profits

enforcement.supervisoryAuthority: National supervisory authority designated by each Member State

fines.description: Pecuniary sanctions based on company turnover

other: Public statement identifying company and nature of infringement

transitionPlan.description: Companies shall adopt and implement a transition plan for climate change mitigation ensuring compatibility with the Paris Agreement 1.5°C target.
requirements: Time-bound targets for 2030 and in 5-year steps up to 2050
Description of decarbonisation levers and key actions
Explanation of investments and funding supporting the plan
Description of role of governance bodies
Plan updated annually
